name: lean-thinking
description: James P. Womack & Daniel T. Jones' Lean Thinking — eliminate waste (muda), maximize customer value, and create wealth in any organization using the 5 lean principles.
useCase: Operational excellence through lean principles — define value, map value streams, create flow, implement pull, and pursue perfection via kaizen across manufacturing, software, healthcare, services, and supply chains.
knownRisks: Lean is often confused with cost-cutting/downsizing; this skill is about creating value, not eliminating jobs
Principles are designed for organizational processes; applying to personal productivity requires adaptation
Transformation requires sustained leadership commitment — one-time implementation rarely works
